title: NERV vs Seele
wikitext:
[]: NERV vs. Seele             Quote Post by Felipe » Thu Dec 13, 2012 8:16 am Forgive me if this is something obvious that I somehow missed, but what was the big contention between Nerv and Seele? Didn't both organizations want to bring Instrumentality about to merge all individual selves into one single Self, thus ending human suffering? This renders EoE, specially, a little bizarre to me, since I don't get what they're fighting over.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Thu Dec 13, 2012 8:29 am Not all Impact/Instrumentality scenarios and outcomes are the same. They both want a form of Instrumentality to occur, but the forms they want to bring about are very different. The precise outcome of Instrumentality depends pretty heavily on the decisions made by the person/soul who is presiding over it. Think of this like the schism between the Catholic and protestant churches hundreds of years ago: both organizations believed in the same basic tenets, but disagreed -- often with violent results -- on a few key points. Gendo's motivations are very personal. He wants to merge himself, Yui, and possibly Rei and Shinji, into a godlike being that can live in eternal peace and roam the cosmos. In this scenario, the rest of humanity can go about its business; they're relatively unimportant to Gendo's designs. Seele's version of Instrumentality is more of a destructive, cleansing-by-fire scenario. They want all of humanity to be merged and sublimated, presumably under the belief that doing so is a necessary step in humanity's evolution into a higher form of life. They take, on faith, that the merged humanity will be a good thing and necessarily will lead to an elevation of some kind. (In reality, this is unclear; it seems more likely that Instrumentality simply pushes a reset button on the human race, so it can be seeded elsewhere in the galaxy and start over from scratch). The series leaves open the questions of whether Instrumentality is a) inevitable, and b) ultimately good or bad for humanity. But the key here is that Instrumentality does not = Instrumentality does not = Instrumentality. There are different possible variants of it (or at least Nerv and Seele seem to believe there are).

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Dec 13, 2012 9:09 am ^ I like this answer. Even though there are points of contention when presenting Gendo's and Seele's plans for Instrumentality as such, that's basically how I think of them. And by "points of contention"; stuff that's popping up in the 'Lilin after...' thread now, or even the Wiki article on the HIP: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13628/The-Lilin-after-Instrumentality/ http://wiki.evageeks.org/Human_Instrumentality_Project

[]:             Quote Post by pioneer » Thu Dec 13, 2012 7:57 pm I'm not sure if you can really say Nerv wanted Instrumentality, but you could definitely say it is Gendo's agenda. From what I can recall, Ritsuko and Naoko seemed to have no interest in HIP, but I'm not sure if they even knew about it. In fact, isn't Nerv's official goal to stop Third Impact? (Not sure what the consensus is on the separateness of an Impact and Instrumentality) Previous to NERV, Naoko Akagi worked on Gehirn. According to the wiki, Gehirn's headquarters were known as "U.N. Artificial Evolution Laboratory." My question here is what is the goal of this 'artificial evolution?" Gehirn created the magi super computers and evangelion. Was eva the end point of evolution or was HIP? But back onto the idea that it isn't Nerv vs Seele, but Gendo vs Seele for their versions of HIP. Yui is the daughter of a prominent member of Seele, so perhaps in essence, it comes down to father, daughter, and son-in-law's versions of HIP in opposition or competition with each other.

[]:             Quote Post by ProbsT » Thu Dec 13, 2012 9:22 pm It's just like pioneer's answer. Summarizing what he said, Gendo wanted to control the Instrumentality at his molds, and then Seele eventually started to question him.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Dec 13, 2012 9:39 pm I'm not sure if you can really say Nerv wanted Instrumentality, but you could definitely say it is Gendo's agenda.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Dec 13, 2012 9:45 pm No, Gendo's plans definitely encompass all of humanity. His thought aren't necessary "I want Yui back, fuck what the rest wants" but "I've lost all hope for this world that took the only good thing I had away from me. That proves that our existence is hopeless as it is. I'm gonna end it." I'd say that ultimately, she is certainly more important to him, but he probably sees no conflict at all between what's best for the world (in his view) and getting Yui back. He probably thinks what he's doing is even in line with Yui's original plans ("Not much longer and all our goals will be archieved") - Before she died, it's implied that he was planning to thwart SEELEs shemes alongside her, but without her guidance, he sorta got his own ideas of what thwarting them encompasses. He just has a different idea of it (Ep 25:*defends the concept in general* "Because it's the only way to live.", EoE: "Death creates nothing!" - He sound almost a bit heroic there, like he intends to protect the world from SEELE. ), which Fuyutsuki seems to consider the lesser of two evils. One could argue about whether his plan is really any better, it's probably only marginally less totally wrong on so many levels, but he sure thinks its significantly better. How exactly their plans differ is hard to guess because it's all metaphysical talk, but some differences are these: Gendo's version -> Involves fusion of adam and lillith, "defies" "God's plan"/ involves humanity gaining some things their creators never intended them to have, and in some way "evolving to a higher form" SEELE's version -> Doesn't involve Adam. Involves the lance. "In tune" with "destiniy" and "Becoming one with god" In terms of metaphysical babble, SEELE sounds mostly like your classic bunch of fanatics, while Gendo is more of a radical transhumanist - both are still very misanthropic and pessimistic in their visions.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Thu Dec 13, 2012 10:07 pm Gendou proposed the Human Instrumentality Project to SEELE a week after Yui died. Gendou framed it as the solution they were looking for all along, while behind the scenes he was planning to use the HIP to reunite with Yui while having control of the process through Rei and the Adam embryo. Gendou was the only one who had the full picture until SEELE became aware of his plans near the end of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Fri Dec 14, 2012 12:03 am True, it's not entirely accurate to conflate Nerv's agenda with Gendo's. I probably shouldn't have used those two designations so interchangeably. I think it's fair to say that the majority of Nerv personnel really do believe their organization exists solely to defeat the angel threat, and, if they learned what Gendo was really up to, would probably think he's crazy. They might even try to stop him. Hence, why Gendo is so secretive about Adam and Lilith, Rei, Central Dogma, and the true nature of the Evangelions. To some extent, even the folks presumably closest to the inner workings of Gendo's plans and facilities -- such as Ritsuko -- seem to have no idea what he's ultimately plotting. (As far as the show makes clear, only Fuyutsuki is in the know). All of that being said, it's probably better to speak of Gendo's vision vs. Seele's vision, rather than Nerv's vision vs. Seele's. Unless we consider Nerv ultimately to be an unwitting puppet/instrument for Gendo's ambitions.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Fri Dec 14, 2012 4:53 am So that would lead into the question of exactly what is the intended scenario for both Gendo and SEELE. Going by end of EoTV and Gendo's "Death creates nothing" Give me the impression that he intended Instrumentality as a temporal therapy or period of forced epiphanies ("the solution to being trapped in one's own skin" as Mr. Tines put it in i don't remember where) while people retain their individualities (and i think that right there is the greatest key difference between the Gendo/SEELE scenarios), i'm not sure of what would happen to humanity after the "Congratulation"-esque epiphanies. Maybe they return to normal, maybe something else. SEELE, however, seemed to desire a complete destruction of individuality and humanity for the creation or advancement towards a higher being, they don't seem to care much about what would happen to the rest of humanity during this "evolution", and are likely convinced it's for their benefit. I would assume their scenario would be something closer to what happened in EoE, but since it wasn't them who were calling the shots at that time, we can never know for sure. They go on into a monologue about how "the time has come" and "throught indiscriminate death and throught prayer" they'll achieve a higher state of being, and Keel blissfully says "All is right with the world" during Komm Susser Todd, so there's that too. I used to believe Gendo only went throught the whole Instrumentality thing just to get Yui back, but i think he would have tried to get involved in it/stop it for his own reasons, likely of a moral nature. But whatever plans he had about Instrumentality before losing Yui were radically affected and changed as he got more and more stricken with grief.

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Fri Dec 14, 2012 10:22 am Dream wrote: So that would lead into the question of exactly what is the intended scenario for both Gendo and SEELE.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Dec 16, 2012 3:05 am Kendrix wrote: No, Gendo's plans definitely encompass all of humanity.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 16, 2012 3:53 am Blue Monday wrote: The series doesn't really give us enough to establish if Gendo intended to tang all of humanity like Seele did.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Dec 16, 2012 4:19 am Ahh, thanks for that. Saves me having to do that "reading up" I mentioned, haha.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Dec 16, 2012 11:23 am Reichu wrote: Difference with Gendo is that HIP was almost certainly going to be a temporary state, and individuality would have been restored one way or another, as opposed to Seele's "crawl back into the womb and stay there" version.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 16, 2012 11:47 am riffraff11235 wrote: So to Gendou the HIP was simply a way to resurrect Yui. The rest of humanity was just collateral damage.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Dec 16, 2012 5:12 pm Reichu wrote: ** According to NGE2, Yui is not "resurrected". Gendo, Shinji, and Rei basically live with her inside the Eva, existing in some kind of idealistic time suspension. [/size]

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Dec 16, 2012 5:15 pm Monk Ed wrote: Shinji and Rei too? Was it just the four of them, or all of humanity presumably in there?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 16, 2012 6:11 pm Looking at it again, it does say that Lilith directs all of humanity's souls into Eva-01. And, inside, Gendo says, "Yui, you taught/showed me the strength to live on as proof that people existed." Seems to lean toward "Eva-01 leaving Earth as fully loaded ark", although can't say for sure until I translate the whole thing...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 17, 2012 12:03 pm riffraff11235 wrote: So to Gendou the HIP was simply a way to resurrect Yui. The rest of humanity was just collateral damage.
